meaning: United Kingdom Atomic Energy Authority 联合王国原子能管理局
examples: Plans for a laser fusion plant have been drawn up at UKAEA in Culham.
The company was part of the UKAEA before being hived off and privatised in 1996.
Returning to the UK, she joined the UKAEA as an energy analyst, modelling alternative strategies for reducing the environmental impacts of energy supply.
Until recently he was Director of the United Kingdom Atomic Energy Authority (UKAEA) Culham, with responsibility for the UK fusion programme and operation of the Joint European Torus (JET).
